Overview  Package  Class   Use  Tree  Deprecated  Index  Help 
 PREV   NEXT FRAMES    NO FRAMES    

Uses of Package
javax.media.mscontrol

Packages that use javax.media.mscontrol
javax.media.mscontrol Provides a java Application Server with a Media Server Control API. 
javax.media.mscontrol.join An application joins media objects to establish media streams between them. 
javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup A MediaGroup is mostly a player/recorder. 
javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup.http Mediagroup parameters specialized for HTTP file play and record 
javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup.signals provides resources for detecting and generating signals: DTMF, speech, .... for any type of media 
javax.media.mscontrol.mixer A MediaMixer has the capacity to combine the media streams coming from multiple sources, into a single media stream. 
javax.media.mscontrol.networkconnection NetworkConnections manage a network termination point, using RTP for an IP network. 
javax.media.mscontrol.resource This package provides the base definitions for managing media processing resources. 
javax.media.mscontrol.resource.common Manage resources that can be inserted in different ResourceContainers like MediaGroups, NetworkConnections or MixerAdapters. 
javax.media.mscontrol.resource.video Capabilites to handle video resources, layout and rendering. 
javax.media.mscontrol.spi This package provides classes and interfaces defining a Driver service provider interface (SPI). 
javax.media.mscontrol.vxml Capabilities to control VoiceXML dialogs that are delegated to the Media Server. 
 

Classes in javax.media.mscontrol used by javax.media.mscontrol
Configuration
          A Configuration designates a particular instance of MediaConfig.
EventType
          An EventType indicates the type of event that a Resource sends.
MediaConfig
          This class defines the internal structure of a ResourceContainer.
MediaConfigException
          The class of things that go wrong during media configuration.
MediaErr
          Definition of the most common errors, as MediaErr instances.
MediaEvent
          An event from a MediaEventNotifier.
MediaEventListener
          To receive events, an application must register a class extending this interface.
MediaException
          MediaException is the parent for [non-Runtime] media Exceptions
MediaObject
          A base class for objects that are created by a Factory, can be released, and can be parameterized.
MediaServiceException
          The class of things that go wrong in MediaService methods.
MediaSession
          A MediaSession is a container and factory for media objects.
MsControlException
          General purpose exception
Parameter
          A Parameter designates a parameter for Resource operation, like Player.MAX_DURATION.
Parameters
          This class is a Map of (Parameter=value) pairs.
Qualifier
          A Qualifier indicates why or how an operation completed.
SupportedFeatures
          Allow to query for the capabilities supported by the implementation.
Value
          A Value is a designator for a particular (enumerated) value of a Parameter.
